channel.title: ainews - Karl's daily AI news
channel.description: Daily AI news, deduped + tagged for hotloop's workflows

item.title: The Press: AI-generated content threatens integrity of NZ general election
item.description: The Press reports a rise in AI-generated fake images, sites and videos of NZ politicians ahead of the general election, citing examples voters are struggling to distinguish from real material. The piece quotes researchers calling for clearer attribution rules and platform takedown processes.

item.title: Ideally acquires NZ AI startup Aether to auto-generate research decks
item.description: Market research platform Ideally has acquired Aotearoa AI startup Aether, which converts raw research data into branded, ready-to-share decks. The deal absorbs Aether’s automation pipeline into Ideally’s core product.

item.title: NZ Herald: Government briefing flags AI risks to Maori culture, stories and data
item.description: A government briefing obtained by the NZ Herald warns that generative AI poses material risks to Maori cultural sovereignty, including misuse of stories, language and identifiable data. Officials flagged the gap between current Crown AI guidance and Maori data governance expectations.

item.title: NZ government expands AI support funding for small businesses
item.description: The New Zealand government announced extra funding to help more small businesses adopt AI tools, and pointed to two new AI offerings from Business Mentors New Zealand. The release positions the spend as part of MBIE’s broader AI strategy for private-sector adoption.

item.title: Spinoff: Datagrid NZ data centre framed as a climate win versus offshore build
item.description: The Spinoff argues Datagrid’s proposed Southland AI data centre would emit far less than an equivalent facility in China, with the gap equivalent to taking around half a million cars off the road each year. Karl-relevant given the ongoing local debate over who actually benefits from the NZ data centre buildout.

item.title: OpenAI previews a personal finance experience in ChatGPT
item.description: OpenAI is rolling out a personal finance preview to ChatGPT Pro users in the US. Subscribers can securely link bank, brokerage and credit accounts to get AI-summarised cashflow, spending breakdowns and tax-aware suggestions inside the chat interface.

item.title: xAI ships Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs
item.description: xAI publishes commercial Grok speech APIs covering streaming STT and TTS with voice cloning. Pricing is positioned beneath comparable OpenAI and ElevenLabs tiers, with API parity for the major Whisper-style endpoints.

item.title: xAI launches Grok Build Early Beta coding CLI
item.description: xAI ships an early beta of Grok Build, a command-line coding agent comparable in surface area to Claude Code and Codex CLI. It runs locally, edits files in place and is initially available to Grok Heavy subscribers.

item.title: Boomi and Red Hat partner on agentic AI stack targeting NZ enterprises
item.description: Boomi and Red Hat announced an integrated agentic AI stack combining Boomi's agent management with Red Hat's OpenShift AI runtime. The companies are pitching the bundle to New Zealand resellers and enterprise customers.

item.title: Sea Limited deploys OpenAI Codex across engineering teams
item.description: Sea Limited CPO David Chen describes rolling out Codex across Shopee and Garena engineering teams to accelerate AI-native development in Southeast Asia. The case study covers internal adoption mechanics, review workflows and the productivity numbers Sea is publishing.

item.title: OpenAI previews Codex inside the ChatGPT mobile app
item.description: OpenAI is previewing Codex inside the ChatGPT mobile app: users can start new coding work, review outputs, steer execution and approve next steps from their phone while the agent keeps running on a laptop, Mac mini or devbox. Greg Brockman framed it as a step toward universal agent usage.

item.title: RNZ: NZ's approach to AI continues to favour 'slop over substance'
item.description: Radio New Zealand argues the government's response to AI remains light on substance, with no minister explicitly accountable for AI policy and a national strategy that defers most hard choices. The piece contrasts NZ with Australia and the UK.

item.title: How the NVIDIA Vera Rubin Platform is Solving Agentic AI Scale-Up
item.description: NVIDIA details how the Vera Rubin platform handles non-deterministic agent inference, where action trajectories vary by run. The blog covers KV-cache management, speculative decoding paths and scheduler changes designed for multi-step tool-using agents at production scale.

item.title: NZ insurers move AI into daily underwriting and claims, with human oversight
item.description: A new CEO survey reports New Zealand insurers are shifting AI out of pilot mode and into day-to-day underwriting and claims processing. Respondents emphasised the role of human judgement on edge cases and explainability.

item.title: Granite Embedding Multilingual R2: Open Apache 2.0 Multilingual Embeddings with 32K Context
item.description: IBM releases Granite Embedding R2, an Apache 2.0 multilingual embedding family supporting 32K context across 17 languages. The release targets enterprise retrieval workloads with improved multilingual recall and matches or beats prior open embedding baselines on MIRACL.

item.title: NVIDIA highlights self-improving agents on RTX PCs and DGX Spark
item.description: NVIDIA shows Nous Research’s self-improving agent stack running on consumer RTX PCs and DGX Spark workstations. The piece walks through tool-use loops, local fine-tuning passes and how desktop hardware now handles workflows that previously needed cloud GPUs.

item.title: Andrew Ng launches Transformers in Practice course with AMD
item.description: DeepLearning.AI’s new Transformers in Practice course, taught by Sharon Zhou and produced with AMD, walks through token-by-token generation, attention, quantization and inference optimisation. Aimed at developers who want intuition for transformer behaviour and deployment trade-offs.

item.title: Anthropic releases free 28-minute workshop on advanced Claude prompting
item.description: Anthropic published a free 28-minute workshop taught by its prompt engineers, covering structured prompting patterns, tool-use design and evaluation for Claude. The session is positioned as the company's canonical training resource.

item.title: Anthropic and Gates Foundation announce $200M AI partnership for health and education
item.description: Anthropic announced a $200 million partnership with the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation to apply AI to global health and education initiatives. The collaboration funds research and deployment of Claude in low-resource settings across both sectors.

item.title: OpenAI reportedly weighing legal action against Apple over strained Siri partnership
item.description: OpenAI is reportedly preparing legal action against Apple following a deterioration in their Siri integration deal. The partnership has stalled over recruiting, privacy expectations and revenue terms, with both sides briefing the press.

item.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Vision, Audio and Language in One Model
item.description: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model that handles vision, audio and language for agentic workflows. The model targets enterprise AI agent stacks running on NVIDIA hardware.

item.title: Adobe and NVIDIA launch autonomous AI agents for creative production with WPP
item.description: Adobe and NVIDIA unveiled a set of autonomous AI agents for creative production, with advertising agency WPP signing on as an early enterprise customer. The agents handle asset generation, editing and review pipelines.

item.title: WSJ: Anthropic's Mythos AI uncovers macOS security flaws that bypass Apple protections
item.description: Anthropic's internal research agent Mythos discovered macOS vulnerabilities capable of bypassing Apple's built-in security mitigations, according to the Wall Street Journal. The findings were responsibly disclosed to Apple ahead of publication.

item.title: OpenAI hit with class action over ChatGPT data sharing via Google/Meta tracking pixels
item.description: A class-action lawsuit filed against OpenAI alleges ChatGPT embedded Meta's Facebook Pixel and Google Analytics tags, sharing user prompt and session data with third parties without consent. The complaint frames this as a wiretap-statute violation.

item.title: Socket's AI scanner flags malware in popular npm package node-ipc within minutes
item.description: Socket reported that its AI-powered supply-chain scanner detected malicious code injected into the widely-used node-ipc npm package within minutes of publication, well ahead of human review. The maintainer's account is believed to have been compromised.

item.title: Hugging Face: Unlocking asynchronicity in continuous batching
item.description: Hugging Face engineers detail how decoupling tokenisation, model forward passes and detokenisation into asynchronous stages lifts throughput in continuous-batching LLM servers. The post includes benchmarks against vLLM and TGI.

item.title: NVIDIA: Accelerated X-Ray Analysis for Nanoscale Imaging (XANI) of Novel Materials
item.description: NVIDIA details a CUDA-accelerated pipeline for X-ray free-electron laser data analysis at nanoscale, enabling researchers to track structural and electron dynamics in fusion materials and semiconductors at production rates rather than days-long batches.

item.title: NVIDIA: Transform Video Into Searchable, Actionable Intelligence with AI Agents and Skills
item.description: NVIDIA released NuRec, a reference workflow that combines vision-language models, multimodal embeddings, and an agent runtime to convert raw video archives into queryable knowledge bases. Aimed at security, manufacturing, and media-archive use cases where humans currently scrub footage manually.

item.title: NVIDIA partners with Ineffable Intelligence to build reinforcement-learning infrastructure
item.description: NVIDIA and startup Ineffable Intelligence announced a joint effort to build the GPU-accelerated training and inference stack RL agents need to scale beyond toy environments. The partnership ships open primitives for environment-step parallelism and trajectory storage targeting frontier-scale RL workloads.

item.title: University of Auckland: NZ's AI data centre boom — who benefits?
item.description: A University of Auckland piece examines Singapore-based Datagrid's planned 'AI factory' data centre near Invercargill, asking how much of the economic value actually accrues to New Zealand versus flowing to the offshore operator. Frames the project against the global hyperscaler land-grab.
